command: dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_clean.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Base; dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_mod.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Win; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "C:\Mount\Win\Users\Default\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup" | Out-Null; Set-Content -Path "C:\Mount\Win\Users\Default\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\init.ps1" -Value "rem placeholder"; Compare-Object (Get-ChildItem -Name "C:\Mount\Base\Users\Default\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) (Get-ChildItem -Name "C:\Mount\Win\Users\Default\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
explanation: Plants a startup script in the Default user profile within the offline image and uses Compare-Object to confirm the file appears only in the modified image.
rationale_steps: Mount the clean and modified images (Admin).
Write a startup script into the offline Default profile of the modified image.
Compare directory listings between baseline and modified with Compare-Object.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with proper semicolons, balanced quotes/parentheses, valid DISM and cmdlet parameters, and parseable Compare-Object expressions.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Mounts WIM images, injects a startup script into the offline image, and compares contents to verify modification—consistent with modifying a system image (T1601), though it requires admin rights and a .ps1 in Startup may not auto-execute by default.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolon separators, valid DISM flags, correct PowerShell cmdlet syntax, and properly escaped paths throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements offline image modification by mounting both a clean and modified WIM image, injecting a startup script into the Default user profile's Startup folder within the modified image, and then using Compare-Object to verify the planted file exists only in the modified image — directly aligning with T1601's technique of modifying system images to introduce persistence.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses correct PowerShell syntax, proper DISM flags, and balanced delimiters throughout the multi-command string.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The logic effectively demonstrates mounting and injecting a persistence script into an offline image, though DISM operations strictly require administrative privileges which contradicts the 'None' privilege metadata.
